task: T5 #143 — Music bed assignments: 6 launch eps + 6 backlog eps + 6 Season 2 concepts + 3 lane samples
note: Beds marked SPEC_ONLY use the fallback_bed_id at publish until the spec bed is sourced/commissioned. Gate 0 row-2 verdict reflects the ASSIGNED bed (fallback if spec). Season 2 eps 13-18 are concept-only; assignments are provisional pending intake and Benjamin's greenlight.

assignments.rationale: Series opener; low-strings prestige tone establishes the Archive formula
Pitch-accent episode benefits from the walking piano motif — movement through an argument
Manuscript beat; Archive Room tone grounds the Beowulf/manuscript focus
Dual-label complexity episode; walking pulse carries the attested/reconstructed argument forward
Single-witness episode; tension pulse underscores the fragility of a language preserved by one book
Deepest-time episode; corpus-walk tone suits the slow accumulation of cuneiform knowledge
Oral-tradition/Vedic episode; tanpura drone texture mirrors the sustained memory transmission the episode describes
assignments.note: Use fallback BED-ANT-001 until BED-VED-001 is sourced/commissioned

assignments.rationale: Revival episode; Aegean/ancient-world lyre resonates with the Near Eastern antiquity of Biblical Hebrew; emotional warmth in the lyre suits the arc's emotional anchor
Babylon and Assyria; reed flute and clay drum are period-accurate Near Eastern instruments matching the cuneiform/Epic of Gilgamesh world
assignments.note: Use fallback BED-ANT-002 until BED-NEAR-001 is sourced/commissioned

assignments.rationale: Bronze Age/ancient Egypt; Temple Column era description (Bronze Age/Hellenistic/Roman) spans the Egyptian period; lyre pulse suits the hieroglyph-visual honesty beat

New World episode; no Mesoamerican period bed currently in library (flagged for future expansion); Archive Room is the neutral-prestige fallback that never conflicts; BED-MESOAM-001 queued for commissioning
assignments.expansion_flag: NEEDS: Mesoamerican/Nahuatl period bed — BED-MESOAM-001 to be commissioned (oc de flores / huehuetl drum texture)

assignments.rationale: Season finale; OCS is the first written Slavic scripture language — Cyril and Methodius / plainchant / medieval liturgical. Scriptorium plainchant is a perfect tonal mirror of the episode's central argument: literacy as rescue
Warehouse Signal thriller tone; darker synth than BED-THR-001, appropriate for narrative sample showcase
Product explainer tone; marimba lift is energetic but not aggressive, suits professional demo context
SFW companion proof; warm acoustic glow is the lane's signature; SageGFE-001 check-in tone
assignments.note: Episode gate is YELLOW (HOLD FOR PUBLISH). Music bed itself is cleared; gate status reflects overall episode hold.

assignments.rationale: Bronze Age Anatolia; Near Eastern reed/drum texture matches the Hittite laryngeal/cuneiform world
assignments.note: Season 2 concept-only; gate verdict provisional until intake run and Benjamin's greenlight. Use fallback BED-ANT-002 at publish.

assignments.rationale: Man'yoshu / Heian court Japanese; koto and shakuhachi texture is period-authentic to the 8th–12th century Japanese court world
assignments.note: Season 2 concept-only; gate verdict provisional. Use fallback BED-DOC-001 at publish until BED-EAST-001 sourced.

assignments.rationale: Pre-Roman Italy; Etruscan flourished c.700–100 BCE — squarely in the Temple Column era description (Bronze Age/Hellenistic/Roman); lyre pulse suits the Rome-alphabet origin narrative

PIE is fully reconstructed — no corpus, no script, no recording. The Archive Room low-strings tone matches the scholarly reconstruction frame; the bed's introspective character suits the episode's honest admission: this is the language no one wrote down

Linear A is a Minoan script from the Aegean Bronze Age — BED-ANT-001 is literally named 'Aegean Dusk' and specced for ancient/Bronze Age; a near-perfect match. The undeciphered/mystery angle suits the dusk character of the track.

Coptic is the liturgical language of the Coptic Orthodox Church; plainchant pad texture mirrors the living liturgical tradition. Pairs with Ep 12 OCS (same bed) — both are scripture-preservation languages still in liturgical use

library_expansion_queue.instrument_brief: Ney reed flute, frame drum (tar or riq), low sustained string. Slow, meditative, ancient. No anachronistic synth.
Tanpura drone (sustained, meditative), very low tabla, optional sitar motif. Vedic/oral-tradition feel. No film-score exoticization.
sourcing_options: commission from South Asian classical musician
library_expansion_queue.priority: MEDIUM — 1 ep on fallback; BED-ANT-001 is acceptable

library_expansion_queue.instrument_brief: Koto (Japanese zither), shakuhachi flute, minimal percussion. Heian court elegance. No synth. No 'generic Asian' clichés.
library_expansion_queue.priority: MEDIUM — 1 ep on fallback; BED-DOC-001 is acceptable

library_expansion_queue.instrument_brief: Huehuetl drum (upright wooden drum), teponaztli (log drum), optional ocarina/wind. Aztec/Nahuatl world. No Hollywood-exoticization.
library_expansion_queue.priority: MEDIUM — 1 ep on BED-DOC-001 fallback; acceptable for concept phase
